T0051.title: Slow Company Flip
T0051.definition: Slow-cooked enterprise refers to a scheme where fraud rings acquire and long-term package shell companies to defraud banks of large tax-invoice loans.
T0051.description: Fraud rings formally transfer the legal representative role of a shell company to a debt mule and wait out the bank's required 3-6 month change-of-ownership period. During this time, they fabricate business transaction records, invoicing, and tax payments to portray the company as a normally operating, high-quality client. This is done to bypass risk models and secure high-limit business loans, after which they default on the debt.

references.title: Black Industry Big Data: The Desperados in Financial Fraud
